title: ITC-SOPI — Independent Television Commission Sense of Presence Inventory
description: 44-item self-report questionnaire measuring sense of presence across four factors. Developed by Lessiter et al. (2001) for cross-media applicability (film, TV, VR, games).
citation: Lessiter, J., Freeman, J., Keogh, E., & Davidoff, J. (2001). A cross-media presence questionnaire: The ITC-Sense of Presence Inventory. Presence, 10(3), 282-297.

SP.name: Sense of Physical Space
SP.description: Feeling of being located in the mediated environment; spatial realism

ENG.name: Engagement
ENG.description: Involvement and attention devoted to the environment

ECO.name: Ecological Validity / Naturalness
ECO.description: How natural and consistent with real-world experience the environment seems

NE.name: Negative Effects
NE.description: Discomfort, disorientation, or adverse reactions (cybersickness indicators)
NE.scoring: sum or mean of items; higher = more negative effects

SP: I had a sense of being in the scenes depicted.
The environment seemed to surround me.
I felt I was visiting the places shown rather than just watching them.
ENG: My attention was captured by the environment.
I became caught up in the environment.
ECO: Events and objects seemed to move and behave in a natural way.
The experience felt consistent with the way things work in real life.

composite_scoring.note: NE subscale is typically reported separately and may be used as a covariate or exclusion criterion rather than summed into a total presence score.
composite_scoring.reverse_coding: NE items may be reverse-coded when computing overall presence composite — check original paper for item-level guidance.
